title: Real Estate Deals in Virginia Beach VA: A Complete Investor's Guide for 2024
excerpt: Virginia Beach is one of Virginia's most active real estate markets, offering strong rental demand, flip opportunities, and wholesale deals year-round. This guide breaks down the best neighborhoods, current market trends, and practical strategies to find profitable deals in Virginia Beach VA.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA. Explore top neighborhoods, market trends, flip & rental strategies, and tips for out-of-state investors.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA, Virginia Beach real estate investing, wholesale deals Virginia Beach, flip houses Virginia Beach, rental properties Virginia Beach, Virginia Beach off-market deals, Virginia Beach real estate market 2024

sections.content: Virginia Beach is not your typical coastal resort town. Yes, it has 35 miles of shoreline and millions of tourists each year — but underneath the vacation vibe is a surprisingly resilient real estate market with real opportunities for investors. Whether you are hunting for wholesale deals, fix-and-flip projects, or long-term rentals, real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA offer something most Sun Belt markets can no longer promise: relative affordability paired with strong demand. If you are new to investing in this region, start with our Real Estate Investing for Beginners guide to build a solid foundation before diving in.
Virginia Beach Real Estate Market Overview

Virginia Beach is the most populous city in Virginia, with over 460,000 residents. That population base creates steady housing demand that does not evaporate when tourist season ends. The local economy is anchored by Naval Station Norfolk — one of the largest naval bases in the world — plus a growing tech corridor, healthcare sector, and tourism industry. This economic diversity keeps vacancy rates low and renter demand high. As of late 2024, the median home price in Virginia Beach sits around $355,000, up roughly 6% from the previous year. That is still well below national coastal market averages, which makes entry points here much more accessible for investors working with limited capital.
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Virginia Beach

Virginia Beach is a large city with distinct micro-markets. Knowing which neighborhoods align with your investment strategy is the difference between a profitable deal and a money pit. Here is a breakdown of the areas investors are watching closely right now.
Oceanfront and Resort Beach Area

The Oceanfront strip and surrounding blocks are prime territory for short-term rental investors. Properties here routinely generate $40,000 to $80,000 in annual gross rental income through platforms like Airbnb and VRBO. Entry prices are higher — expect $450,000 and up for a decent condo or townhome — but the revenue potential justifies the price for investors who understand vacation rental management. Check local zoning ordinances before you buy, as Virginia Beach has specific short-term rental permit requirements.
Bayside and Chesapeake Beach

Bayside is one of the most investor-friendly neighborhoods in the city for buy-and-hold strategies. Median home prices hover around $290,000 to $320,000, and the military tenant pool — thanks to proximity to military bases — keeps vacancy rates extremely low. Long-term rents typically range from $1,500 to $2,200 per month for a three-bedroom single-family home, which produces solid cash flow at today's prices when purchased right.
Seatack and the Oceana Area

For wholesalers and flippers, the Seatack neighborhood and areas surrounding Naval Air Station Oceana offer some of the best distressed deal opportunities in the city. Home prices in these pockets can still be found in the $200,000 to $280,000 range, and after-repair values on renovated properties often push $340,000 to $390,000. That spread leaves room for both assignment fees on wholesale deals and healthy flip margins.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Virginia Beach
Wholesale Deals

Wholesaling is active in Virginia Beach, particularly targeting older military housing stock built in the 1960s through 1980s. These properties are often owned by aging landlords, out-of-state heirs from estate sales, or military families who relocated and could not sell quickly. The key to wholesaling here is building relationships with probate attorneys, connecting with local real estate agents who handle estate sales, and running direct mail campaigns to absentee owners. Learn the fundamentals in our Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners guide. Before assigning any contract, run the numbers using our free Wholesale Deal Calculator to confirm your MAO (maximum allowable offer) and protect your buyer's margin.
Fix-and-Flip Opportunities

Virginia Beach flippers are finding the best margins in mid-tier neighborhoods where cosmetic rehabs — new kitchens, updated bathrooms, fresh landscaping — can add $50,000 to $80,000 in value relatively quickly. The city's consistent buyer demand, fueled by military relocations (called PCS moves), means flipped homes in move-in ready condition sell fast. Average days on market for updated homes in desirable zip codes like 23464 and 23462 is often under 30 days. To nail your numbers before you make an offer, use our free ARV Calculator to estimate your after-repair value accurately.
Long-Term and Short-Term Rentals

The rental market in Virginia Beach is one of its strongest investment cases. The military community alone generates thousands of renter households annually as service members rotate in and out of the area on 2-to-3 year orders. This creates a predictable, high-quality tenant base. Long-term rentals in Bayside, Kempsville, and Pembroke typically cash flow between $200 and $500 per month after PITI when purchased below $320,000. On the short-term side, oceanfront condos and beach cottages can generate returns that long-term rentals simply cannot match — but they require more active management.

Pro Tip: Target military landlords who are being reassigned and need to sell quickly. Run a direct mail campaign to owners in military-heavy zip codes (23451, 23453, 23464) using absentee owner lists. These sellers are often motivated and flexible on price because they are working against a hard move-out deadline.
Local Market Trends Investors Need to Know

Several trends are shaping where smart money is moving in Virginia Beach right now. First, inventory remains historically low — active listings are roughly 30% below pre-pandemic levels — which means competition for listed properties is fierce. This makes off-market deal sourcing more important than ever. Learning how to find off-market real estate deals is an essential skill in this environment. Second, interest rate pressure has cooled some flippers who relied on cheap debt, but it has also motivated more sellers who are stuck in properties they cannot refinance. Third, the city's Tech Center development project and ongoing investment in the Dome site redevelopment are adding long-term appreciation tailwinds to properties in the central Virginia Beach corridor.

"Virginia Beach is unique because the military creates a constant churn of buyers and renters that most markets simply do not have. That cycle never really stops, which is why smart investors who understand this market keep coming back." — Experienced Virginia Beach real estate investor
Practical Tips for Out-of-State Investors

Virginia Beach is a popular target for out-of-state investors, particularly from the Northeast corridor and from military families who served here and want to own a piece of the market they know well. If you are investing remotely, here is how to approach this market effectively.

items: Build a local power team first: Find a property manager, a licensed contractor, and a real estate agent who works with investors before you make your first offer. A bad contractor selection is the number one profit killer for out-of-state flippers.

Understand flood zone designations: A significant portion of Virginia Beach properties sit in FEMA flood zones. Flood insurance can add $1,500 to $4,000 per year to your holding costs. Always check the flood map before running your deal analysis.

Use virtual wholesalers or bird dogs: Many local wholesalers in Virginia Beach actively market deals to out-of-state cash buyers. Get on their lists early and build a reputation as a fast, reliable closer.
Analyze deals conservatively: Remote rehab management always costs more than local. Add a 10-15% buffer to your estimated repair costs when projecting flip profits or rental returns.

Study the military rental market: Research BAH (Basic Allowance for Housing) rates for the Virginia Beach area. This is the monthly housing stipend military members receive, and it effectively sets the ceiling for what military renters will pay. For 2024, E-5 to O-3 BAH rates in the Virginia Beach area range from $1,800 to $2,700 per month — useful benchmarks for pricing your rentals.

Leverage data-driven platforms: Use tools like those available at AIWholesail's Virginia Beach market page to find motivated sellers and distressed properties without needing boots on the ground every day.

sections.content: Pro Tip: Before submitting any offer in Virginia Beach — flip, wholesale, or rental — run it through a full deal analysis framework. Our guide on how to analyze real estate deals like a pro walks you through every number you need to check, from ARV to net cash-on-cash return.
Is Virginia Beach Right for Your Investment Strategy?

Virginia Beach is not the cheapest market in the country, nor is it the most explosive for appreciation. What it offers is something rarer: stability. A military-anchored economy, consistent rental demand, a growing tech sector, and a tourism industry that adds short-term rental income potential make this city a balanced market for multiple strategies. Wholesalers will find distressed deals in aging military housing stock. Flippers will find healthy ARV spreads in mid-tier neighborhoods. Long-term rental investors will find a reliable, quality tenant base. And short-term rental operators will find one of the most visited beaches on the East Coast. The key is knowing your numbers, building the right local team, and sourcing deals before they hit the MLS.
